title: 'Cricket connection' surfaces in Pune murder case; Siya's parents deny knowing affair with Chetan
content: An investigation into businessman Ketan Agarwal's death has uncovered new details about prime accused Siya Goyal's alleged relationship with Chetan Chaudhary. Police suspect Siya's brother, Sahil Goyal, knew about the affair, while Siya's father denies any prior knowledge of her relationship or reluctance towards her engagement to Ketan. Both Siya and Chetan are in police custody as the probe continues.

title: After monkey scare, newlywed woman dies after falling from temple hill in Tamil Nadu
content: A tragic incident unfolded at the Kalugumalai Ucchi Pillayar temple in Tuticorin where a newlywed woman, Anitha, 24, lost her life. While feeding monkeys on Friday evening, she reportedly panicked when a group surrounded her. In her haste to escape, she fell approximately 120 feet from the hilltop, succumbing to her injuries at the scene. Police are investigating the unfortunate event.

title: Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi make his India debut in 2nd T20I vs Ireland?
content: Excitement mounts for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential India debut in the second T20I against Ireland. Despite his talent, the team management, including captain Shreyas Iyer and batting coach Sitanshu Kotak, opted for experienced players in the series opener. While Sooryavanshi's chance is anticipated, the focus remains on balancing opportunities with rewarding in-form players.

title: 'Let him serve the team': Ashwin's massive statement on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut delay
content: Despite anticipation for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut, India's management retained their established opening pair against Ireland. Veteran spinner Ravi Ashwin endorsed this, emphasizing the learning value of observing from the sidelines. He stressed that rushing the young talent isn't beneficial, highlighting that patience and understanding team dynamics are crucial for a long, successful career, even if it means serving in a support role initially.

title: Anthropic says US restored Mythos 5 access: but here's why the ban the world protested remains
content: The US government has let Anthropic switch Mythos 5 back on, but only for roughly 100 vetted organizations defending critical infrastructure. The public-facing Fable 5 stays offline with no timeline, and the two-week export ban remains in force for everyone else. Critics, including Sam Altman, warn that Washington is now picking which companies get frontier AI—a precedent OpenAI's own restricted GPT-5.6 rollout mirrors.

title: '4kg silver brick donated by Sena (UBT) missing': Sanjay Raut amid Ram Temple funds row
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut has raised serious concerns over a missing 4 kg silver brick and a Rs 12 crore donation made by Uddhav Thackeray for the Ram Mandir in Ayodhya. Raut demanded a full investigation into the alleged irregularities, citing a lack of receipts or updates from the Trust.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: Couple denied boarding despite valid passes, spent night at airport; airline to pay Rs 62,000
content: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: How drones could transform logistics on India's Himalayan frontier
content: The Indian Army is revolutionizing high-altitude logistics with drones, offering a cost-effective and faster alternative to helicopters and foot transport. New drone platforms are being developed to meet stringent requirements for payload and altitude, with domestic manufacturers stepping up. Initiatives like HIM-DRONE-A-THON are fostering innovation, aiming to equip frontline troops with reliable aerial resupply capabilities in challenging Himalayan terrains, bolstering India's self-reliance in defence.

title: 'He wore wig': Siya Goyal tells cops why she disliked fiancé Ketan Agarwal
content: A shocking revelation emerged in the Lohagad Fort murder case as accused Siya Goyal reportedly told police she disliked her fiancé, Ketan Agarwal, due to his hair patch. Investigators are also probing her brother's knowledge of her alleged affair with co-accused Chetan Chaudhary. Financial disparities between families are believed to have influenced the engagement, with Siya allegedly fearing family disappointment more than plotting murder.

title: Oracle layoffs: 500+ employees get emails from leadership, calls them 'proposed impacted employee'
content: Oracle has begun a second round of layoffs in Romania, notifying around 500 to 520 employees on June 25 with emails headed "collective dismissal-you are a proposed impacted employee." The cuts span OCI, Database Technologies, Oracle Health and several other units, and were reportedly planned months ago. They form part of Oracle's global drive to shed payroll and fund its debt-heavy AI data centre push.

title: From Crimea to Volgograd: Inside Ukraine's 40-day strike campaign against Russia
content: In a bold move, Ukraine has commenced a 40-day drone campaign targeting key Russian infrastructure to heighten pressure on Moscow. Announced by President Zelensky, the offensive kicked off with a significant strike affecting numerous Russian regions. Critical assets including naval vessels and air defense systems were hit, leading to injuries and economic setbacks, especially in Crimea.

title: 'BJP used Kanhaiya Lal murder for votes': Ashok Gehlot says justice still elusive four years later
content: Former Rajasthan CM Ashok Gehlot criticizes the BJP, alleging justice is delayed in the Kanhaiya Lal murder case despite a four-year lapse. He claims only 21 of 180 witnesses have testified, suggesting deliberate stalling for electoral advantage. Gehlot points out the absence of the issue in Prime Minister Modi's recent Rajasthan visits, urging voters to recognize the BJP's alleged political maneuvering over genuine justice for the victim's family.

title: Rohit Sharma's 2027 World Cup place? Manjrekar makes bold statement
content: Rohit Sharma is back for India's ODI series against England, sparking debate about his 2027 World Cup prospects. Former player Sanjay Manjrekar advocates for prioritizing younger talent for the future of Indian cricket, suggesting Rohit might not be the ideal choice for the next World Cup squad. Virat Kohli also returns, pending fitness. The squad features a mix of experienced and new faces for the upcoming series.

title: Watch: California security guard fires Taser into man's face at point-blank range, arrested
content: In a shocking incident outside a Carlsbad bar during a soccer watch party, a security guard has been charged after reportedly tasering a man in the face. The altercation was captured on video, revealing the man collapsing following the shock. Police have arrested 42-year-old David Marquez on charges of assault with a deadly weapon and stun gun assault. The victim is receiving medical care, and investigations are still underway.

title: Op Tiger 3.0 on, 14 UBT MLAs will defect, claims Sena mantri
content: Shiv Sena minister Gulabrao Patil claimed 'Operation Tiger 3.0' is underway, with 14 Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs set to join Eknath Shinde's faction. However, another minister, Uday Samant, downplayed the operation but admitted MVA MLAs are interested in joining Shiv Sena. Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray is touring constituencies of recently defected MPs, with his party's Vidarbha MLAs pledging support for his upcoming rally.

title: Fadnavis and Uddhav Thackeray share Mumbai-Nagpur flight
content: Political rivals Devendra Fadnavis and Uddhav Thackeray shared a flight from Mumbai to Nagpur, exchanging brief pleasantries. Thackeray, accompanied by his son Aaditya and Sanjay Raut, is embarking on a three-day tour of Vidarbha and Marathwada to engage with party workers in constituencies held by rebel MPs. This unexpected encounter on a regular service flight was described as a mere coincidence by party sources.

title: AMCA engine talks hit roadblock, US company raises cost threefold
content: India's ambitious fifth-generation fighter jet program, the AMCA, faces a significant hurdle as the cost of General Electric's F414 engines has tripled, potentially escalating the project's budget. With the airframe design finalized around these engines and limited alternatives, India may be forced to accept the higher price. This development impacts the timeline for the fighter, which is crucial for maintaining air superiority against regional rivals.

title: Khan Sir's arrest protection extended till June 30 as Patna court defers hearing
content: A Patna court has decided to extend the interim protection from arrest for the renowned educator Faisal Khan, widely known as Khan Sir, amidst a dramatic shooting incident linked to his coaching institute. The incident involves claims of vandalism and gunfire allegedly instigated by his security guards under Khan Sir's instructions. Final arguments are set for June 30, and Khan Sir is reported to be fully cooperative with investigators.

title: 'India deserves permanent UNSC seat': Seychelles backs New Delhi's bid ahead of PM Modi's visit
content: Seychelles Foreign Minister Barry Faure has strongly backed India's bid for a permanent seat on the UN Security Council, citing New Delhi's significant global stature, population, and contributions to international peace. Faure emphasized the need for reforms in global institutions to reflect current geopolitical realities, advocating for better representation for countries like India and regions such as Africa. He described the India-Seychelles relationship as an excellent, long-standing strategic partnership.

title: Who was K Bhagyaraj? Director of 25+ films and actor who worked with Amitabh
content: Veteran filmmaker and actor K. Bhagyaraj, celebrated for his unique blend of humor, romance, and family emotions, has passed away at 73. Beginning his career as an assistant director, Bhagyaraj went on to direct over 25 successful films, including 'Mundhanai Mudichu' and 'Andha 7 Naatkal'. He also made a significant impact in Hindi cinema with remakes like 'Aakhree Raasta'.

title: CCTV captures man thrashing elderly father; video sparks ire
content: A shocking CCTV video has emerged from Jaipur, showing a 67-year-old retired BSNL employee being brutally assaulted by his son. The victim sustained severe head and facial injuries during the relentless attack.  The incident, which occurred on June 19, has sparked public outrage, with the family alleging initial delays in police action. An FIR was registered after the victim revisited the station with his daughters.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' box office Day 2 [LIVE]: Akshay starrer is eyeing Rs 40 crore worldwide
content: With its nostalgic charm and engaging humor, 'Welcome To The Jungle' has struck a chord with viewers, leading to a commendable opening of Rs 15 crore on day one, thanks to a star-studded cast led by Akshay Kumar. While a few skeptics highlight its originality issues, the captivating performances of seasoned actors Farida Jalal and Kiran Kumar have been praised as major highlights of the film.

title: Russia-Ukraine war: When drones get too close, these bullets become soldier's final shield
content: Frontline soldiers are now equipped with specialized anti-drone bullets, a crucial last line of defense against the escalating threat of unmanned aerial systems. These innovative rounds, designed to fragment and damage drones at close range, empower infantry to directly counter reconnaissance and kamikaze strikes. India's ARDE and private firms are developing these vital munitions, ensuring even small units can defend against aerial threats in modern warfare.

title: Lohagarh murder case: Pune police examine car used by Ketan Agarwal during fatal trekking trip
content: Pune police are investigating the red electric car used by murder victim Ketan Agarwal on his final trip to Lohagad Fort. His fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her friend Chetan Chaudhary are in custody. The Maharashtra government has approved a fast-track court and appointed Ujjwal Nikam as special public prosecutor following a plea for swift justice from Agarwal's father, who seeks the death penalty for the accused.

title: Why the US is delivering F-35s without radars: Inside the AN/APG-85 radar
content: The US military is accepting F-35 fighter jets without their advanced AN/APG-85 radar due to development delays. These aircraft, fitted with ballast, will be retrofitted later. This unusual move by the Pentagon aims to maintain production momentum for the F-35's Block 4 modernization, which includes significant upgrades to sensors and capabilities. The new radar is crucial for future threat engagement.

title: Punjab implements VB-G RAM G scheme months after opposing it
content: Punjab has officially implemented the Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Scheme, effective July 1, despite earlier unanimous opposition from its assembly. This new rural employment law, replacing MGNREGA, extends the guaranteed wage employment to 125 days annually for rural households. The state's notification signifies a policy shift, aligning with the Centre's vision despite previous concerns over funding and impact on Dalit labourers.

title: 'Was crushing tablet for daughter': TN minister's old IPL video sparks drug use allegations
content: Tamil Nadu Minister D. Sarathkumar has vehemently denied drug use allegations following the resurfacing of a two-year-old video. He clarified that the footage, showing him arranging a powder, depicted him crushing a tablet for his daughter. Sarathkumar dismissed the claims as politically motivated and baseless, asserting his readiness to cooperate with any investigation into the matter.

title: 'He wanted to give me early retirement': Father mourns son killed in Mumbai local train stabbing
content: Virar mourned the tragic loss of 21-year-old Mayank Lohar, brutally murdered on a local train. His grieving family, devastated by the senseless violence, seeks the harshest punishment for the accused. Lohar, remembered as a bright young man with aspirations for his family's future, was a victim of a fatal altercation. Authorities are reviewing security measures following the incident.

title: 'Siya Goyal's wig remark, forced marriage, secret relationship': Twist after twist in murder case
content: A Lohagad Fort fall has escalated into a murder probe, with police arresting a fiancée and her alleged partner for the realtor's death. Investigators suggest a complex motive involving a secret relationship, reluctance to marry, and family pressure. Evidence points to a conspiracy, with conflicting accounts from the accused on who masterminded the alleged crime. The investigation continues.

title: No debut, but loud cheers: Belfast crowd shows love for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut is still pending, but the focus shifted to Ireland's historic 34-run victory in the first T20I.  Despite the loss, Shreyas Iyer vowed a strong comeback. Ireland's debutants impressed, with Jai Moondra and Matt Hollard making immediate impacts, while Abhishek Sharma's quickfire fifty wasn't enough for India.

title: 'Gravest sin': Priyanka Gandhi says Ram Temple donation fraud has 'stunned entire nation'
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra has called for a thorough investigation into the alleged irregularities surrounding donations to the Ram Temple. She highlighted the need for severe consequences for those responsible and raised concerns about whether lower-tier staff could act independently without higher-ups' influence. Gandhi asserted that the inquiry should be transparent and hold all involved parties accountable for their actions.

title: D4vd murder case: Prosecutors reveal alleged motive before July hearing
content: The criminal case involving singer D4vd remains active in California. Prosecutors allege he killed 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez to stop her from exposing an alleged relationship, while the defense strongly denies those claims. Burke has pleaded not guilty, and the court will continue hearing the case, with a preliminary hearing scheduled for July 21, 2026.

title: Watch: Venezuela's twin earthquakes leave nearly 1,000 dead, trail of despair
content: Venezuela reels from twin earthquakes, with the death toll tragically climbing to 920 and over 50,000 reported missing amidst widespread devastation. Rescue efforts are in full swing, with international aid pouring in from nations like the US and India, which launched Operation Amistad. The humanitarian crisis deepens, particularly for the millions of children affected, as the search for survivors continues under mountains of rubble.

title: ‘Accused’s ego was hurt’: Passenger recalls 21-year-old getting stabbed to death in Mumbai local
content: A horrific murder unfolded on a Mumbai local train after a heated argument over a compartment door escalated into a fatal stabbing. Eyewitnesses recount the brutal attack on 21-year-old Mayank Lohar by Roshan Suvarna, who allegedly brandished a knife at other passengers before fleeing. The incident has left commuters traumatized, with calls for the strictest punishment for the accused.

title: Man electrocuted during Bengaluru Metro lighting work
content: Tragedy struck near Trinity Circle as a labourer, Gurappa, was electrocuted while working on Namma Metro pillar lighting. He accidentally struck an underground live wire during excavation. Meanwhile, a woman's chain caused a brief disruption on the Purple Line, delaying services. BMRCL also announced early Metro operations on June 27 to support a BDA plantation drive and walkathon.

title: 'We failed': Abhishek Sharma explains India's shock defeat to Ireland
content: Ireland secured a historic 34-run victory over reigning T20 World Cup champions India in the first T20I at Belfast. Despite a blistering 50 off 20 balls from Abhishek Sharma, India faltered, failing to adapt to the conditions. Ireland posted 182/9, with Lorcan Tucker leading the charge. India were bowled out for 148, giving Ireland a 1-0 series lead.

title: FIFA World Cup: Spain book Round of 32 spot; Uruguay out
content: Spain topped Group H with a 1-0 win over Uruguay, secured by Álex Baena’s first-half goal after a costly Fernando Muslera error. Despite Uruguay’s late pressure and a few clear chances, Spain’s control and discipline proved decisive. The result sends Spain into the Round of 32 unbeaten, while Uruguay exit after a wasteful, inconsistent group-stage campaign.

title: K. Bhagyaraj: the quintessential man-next-door hero of Tamil cinema
content: As a bespectacled hero, Bhagyaraj cut through the preconceived notions of how a celluloid figure should appear

title: Sonia Gandhi hits out at Modi government’s ‘stony silence’ on Gaza ‘genocide’
content: Amidst the growing public backlash against Israel and the international community’s cognisance of the unjustifiable brutality unleashed on Gaza, India remains a lone voice of silence, says Congress leader Sonia Gandhi

title: Bhagyaraj passes away | Live updates: TN to accord state honours, says CM Vijay
content: He began his film career as an assistant director to veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja on the 1977 film 16 Vayathinile.

title: ‘Great loss to Indian cinema’: Condolences pour in for actor-director Bhagyaraj
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay said the veteran filmmaker and actor will be laid to rest with full State honours

title: Ashok Gehlot questions ‘elusive’ justice in Kanhaiya Lal murder case, says BJP used issue for elections
content: The Congress leader stated that despite the NIA investigating and a special court hearing the case, there has been no significant progress under BJP governments at the Centre and State

title: Politics: where the versatile Bhagyaraj could not make a dent
content: Actor-director K. Bhagyaraj passed away following a heart attack on Saturday; known for his versatility in the film world, Mr. Bhagyaraj, however, had a political stint that did not take off

title: Over 150 Nihangs camping at Paonta Sahib gurdwara, say won’t leave until arrested members released
content: On June 16, a dispute in a Uttarakhand market between residents and Nihangs escalated into violence, injuring several locals and one Nihang. Police later arrested four Nihangs and registered a case

title: Vijayawada division loaded 40.64 million tonnes of freight in 2025-26, earned ₹4,136 crore
content: The Divisional Railway Manager asked customers for new logistics ideas and sought CCTVs at all goods sheds, citing ₹4,136 crore in freight earnings last year

title: Patna court extends Khan Sir's protection from arrest till June 30 in coaching firing case
content: The case relates to a firing incident that occurred in early June after Khan Sir's coaching institute was allegedly vandalised by a group of miscreants. His security guards are accused of opening fire during the incident.

title: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann’s fake video controversy | Explained
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann has been embroiled in a controversy over a fake video. The Hindu explains the reason for the controversy, a new twist, the Chief Minister’s defence, and the road ahead for the AAP ahead of the 2027 Assembly election

title: Nearly 6 crore names have been deleted in first yearof SIR of electoral rolls
content: Post Bihar SIR, its voters' list was pruned by nearly 65 lakh amid allegations by the Opposition and activists that the poll authority was working at the behest of the BJP to disenfranchise citizens for want of documents

title: K. Bhagyaraj, prominent actor and director, passes away
content: Hospital sources told The Hindu that he suffered a cardiopulmonary arrest and could not be resuscitated

title: Why is India’s humanitarian aid in response to Venezuelan earthquake called Operation Amistad?
content: The name is derived from the Spanish word amistad, meaning friendship, reflecting India’s solidarity with the South American nation in a time of crisis

title: Stay away from ganja and drugs, A.P. Home Minister V. Anitha tells youth
content: Marking the International Day Against Drug Abuse, the Minister joined a Nasha Mukt Bharat event, gave away awards to 56 officers and launched a new app

title: Ayisha Elizabeth, wife of late Malayalam scriptwriter John Paul, passes away in Kochi
content: She was admitted to a private hospital in Kochi on June 25, after sustaining a head injury in an accident while out for a walk

title: Unqualified practitioners found administering steroids, painkillers at three clinics in Hyderabad
content: The Council appealed to citizens to report suspected quacks through its WhatsApp helpline at 9154382727

title: Explained | The Telecommunications Act, 2023 comes into force
content: While the law came into force almost three years ago, the subordinate rules needed to give it force of law have been under consultation until now

title: Mumbai local train murder accused confesses to stabbing; remanded till June 29
content: According to his statement, Lohar asked him to close the train door because rainwater was entering the compartment. The two got into an argument, during which Lohar punched Suvarna.

title: Assam includes tea landholding in farmers’ registry portal
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the facility will be a game-changer for lakhs of small tea growers in the State

title: The many faces of actor-auteur Bhagyaraj
content: Terrific with his anecdotes, Bhagyaraj still offers a glimpse into the way the Tamil film industry functioned in the 1970s, ‘80s, and ‘90s

title: What is the Ram Temple embezzlement case | Explainer
content: The FIR alleges a coordinated theft operation involving these temple personnel and cash-counting staff systematically pilfered cash from donation boxes and hid temple valuables during the counting and sorting phases

title: Telangana gears up for Pulse Polio drive on June 28, targeting 40.97 lakh children
content: The Minister said the vaccination drive should cover every child through fixed booths, mobile teams and door-to-door survey

title: Outgoing Telangana CS Ramakrishna Rao joins ranks of bureaucrats stepping in as Advisors
content: Operating each advisor’s office costs taxpayers roughly ₹2.5 lakh monthly. Civil Societies laments the appointments as an unconstitutional drain of public money. In-service bureaucrats are apprehensive that such appointments could create another level of hierarchy in administration.

title: Explained | Uttarakhand Nihang Sikh Controversy
content: What began as a parking dispute involving a group of Nihang Sikhs, within days, turned into one of the state’s biggest law-and-order challenges during this year’s Char Dham and Hemkund Sahib pilgrimage season.

title: PM Modi leaves for Seychelles to attend golden jubilee of country's National Day
content: "Together, we will work to advance the progress of our peoples and promote security and prosperity in the Indian Ocean region," says PM Modi

title: Pune fort murder: Siya's mother seeks harshest punishment; government appoints special prosecutor
content: Pooja Goyal said she was shocked after the incident. "The pain we feel is more than what they feel. Whoever is guilty should receive the harshest punishment," she reiterated.

title: Explained | Netra AEW&C: India’s indigenous ‘eye in the sky’ that strengthens air power
content: India is the fifth country in the world to develop this capability, as the Netra can detect, track, identify and monitor airborne and maritime targets enhancing the network-centric operations capabilities of the IAF

title: Spinal Muscular Atrophy drug: regulatory approval should guide Kerala’s procurement policy, say experts
content: Kerala cannot continue to procure the original molecule at high prices when an approved generic formulation is available at a fraction of the price, says B. Ekbal

title: Government proposes simplifying procedure for import of drugs for examination, test, or analysis under Drugs Rules, 1945
content: The Ministry has already carried out amendments to the New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules, 2019 in January 2026 introducing a similar notification system for domestic test licences

title: U.S. President takes to social media to thank India for ‘Donald Trump Avenue’ in Hyderabad
content: The plan to name Donald Trump Avenue was spelt out ahead of the two-day ‘Telangana Rising Global Summit’ held in a newly carved out plan of Future City in December 2025.

title: Motorcyclist critical after tree branch falls on his head in Bengaluru
content: The victim, identified as Satish, was on his way for finance collection work when a dry branch from a roadside tree suddenly broke off and fell on his head

title: Champat Rai: The RSS pracharak at the centre of the Ram temple donation row
content: After decades as a low-profile organiser working behind the scenes, Mr. Rai has been thrust into an unwelcome spotlight amid corruption charges and rumours of his resignation from the Ram temple trust

title: A convert to Islam cannot claim the status of Backward Class Muslim, rules Madras High Court
content: Categorising certain sects as Backward and the remaining as Forward is antithetical to Quranic injunctions. Islam seeks to establish an egalitarian society, says Madurai Bench

title: Centre asks hospitals to disclose post-organ transplant survival data
content: Registered transplant hospitals across the country were told to display the crucial data on the home page of their respective websites

title: Beijing’s ties with Bangladesh does not target any third party, says Chinese Foreign Ministry
content: China expressed support to Bangladesh to take up the comprehensive Teesta river project; Rahman conveys Dhaka’s commitment to ‘one China’ policy; earlier China backed Bangladesh in supporting its ‘sovereignty and territorial integrity’

title: All eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case sent to judicial custody
content: Despite rumours that Champat Rai has resigned as general secretary of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, no official confirmation yet; arrested accused include close aides of Mr. Champat Rai

title: India launches ‘Op Amistad’ to aid quake-hit Venezuela; sends relief material, medical assistance
content: The 41-member team, comprising experienced rescue personnel and medical professionals, will work closely with the Venezuelan authorities in search and rescue operations

title: Cleaning Godavari a challenge ahead of 2027 Pushkarams
content: The waters of the sacred river of Akhanda Godavari have been declared unsuitable either for drinking or a holy dip due to contamination with effluents being released from industries and untreated sewage water, threatening the survival of the river ecosystem and forcing four lakh residents of the city to drink, while traditional fisherfolk abandoned the river for drinking, bathing on the Eastern bank.

title: Congress appoints Rajendra Pal Gautam, Sanjay Dutt as AICC in-charge for UP, Haryana
content: Mr. Gautam replaces Avinash Pande in Uttar Pradesh, while Mr. Dutt replaces senior party leader Hariprasad in Haryana

title: Exhibition to pay tribute to legacy of Narasimha Rao
content: The event will include an exhibition showcasing a collection of rare photographs, official documents, speeches, correspondence and archival material tracing the journey of the late former PM.

title: One dead in Agartala blast; State govt. orders probe
content: The fact-finding committee, led by the Secretary, Urban Develop Department, has been directed to submit a report within seven days

title: Kuki-Zo Council’s apology for killings is not enough: Manipur Naga youth body
content: The All Naga Students’ Association, Manipur, slams the Kuki-Zo Council for using a derogatory term while referring to the slain Liangmai Naga civilians

title: West Bengal Congress to step up protests over NTA question paper leaks
content: Congress leadership in West Bengal will hold protests on August 1 demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan as the part of nation wide programme ‘Chhatron Ki Goonj’ said Congress leader Prasenjit Bose

title: Pharma firm licence revoked over Kota post-partum deaths; WHO seeks report
content: Five women died between May 5 and May 17 at the JK Lone Hospital and the Super Speciality Wing of Government Medical College in Kota, Rajasthan, following Caesarean section deliveries

title: Supreme Court lays to rest 30-year-old case of man’s death over a ₹500 watch
content: The top court Bench headed by Justice Ujjal Bhuyan closed criminal proceedings in the culpable homicide case after noting that two of the three accused persons have died during the pendency of the appeal

title: NFSA amendments: 7 kg of foodgrains for a person, and maximum 35 kg for a household
content: The Centre has proposed revising the foodgrain entitlement for AAY beneficiaries under the NFSA. While it says the change will ensure fairer distribution, critics fear it could reduce allocations for many households

title: Himachal should be given 7.19 % share in Chandigarh: Sukhu
content: Chief Minister raises Chandigarh share, BBMB dues and Shanan project during meeting with Punjab Governor and UT Administrator

title: UCC to be implemented in Bengal through proper procedure, says Suvendu
content: Implementation of UCC was promised by Union Home Minister Amit Shah during the campaign for West Bengal Assembly election.

title: Myanmar replaces Afghanistan as key opium source, impact seen on India’s eastern border: NCB
content: Myanmar’s Golden Triangle has expanded as an opiate supplier and meth hub, with the drugs then flowing across a porous border into Manipur, Mizoram, and Nagaland; drone-based drug trafficking from Pakistan is also a rising concern

title: The rules that failed 15 lives in Lucknow
content: On June 22, a fire broke out in a building in Uttar Pradesh’s capital city, claiming 15 lives, most of them students and staff of an animation centre. According to eyewitnesses, bystanders, and survivors, the building lacked basic fire safety systems. Mayank Kumar reports on the tragedy and the regulatory gaps it exposes amid rapid urbanisation in India’s most populous State

title: Extension of Kochi Water Metro ferries to Kadamakudy by Onam
content: Relocation of Chinese fishing nets along the route and dredging of the terminal premises are the remaining works before services can begin, says KWML chief

title: Inside Lives Of Delhi's Pavement Dwellers And The Dangers They Face
content: A 10-year-old girl sleeping with her family on a pavement was allegedly kidnapped, raped and murdered in Delhi.

title: Telangana Man Watched 'How To Kill' Videos, Then Killed Family Of 4 Over Debt
content: After the killings, the accused fled in a car, stealing cash, gold and property documents. They later pledged the stolen jewellery for Rs 5.30 lakh.

title: Cricket Angle In Pune Fort Murder Case As Siya Goyal's Brother Makes New Revelations
content: Siya Goyal is accused of pushing her fiance, Ketan Agarwal, to his death from a cliff at Lohagad Fort, with help from her lover Chetan Chaudhary.

title: PM Modi To Meet World's Oldest Living Land Animal During Seychelles Visit
content: This visit is expected to consolidate cooperation between India and Seychelles in areas of maritime security, blue economy, climate resilience and capacity building.

title: "If She Didn't Like Ketan...": Pune Fort Murder Accused's Brother To Cops
content: Police allege that Ketan Agarwal was pushed off a cliff at Lohagad Fort on June 18 by his fiance Siya Goyal, 20 and her alleged lover Chetan Chaudhary, 22.

title: "We're Hoping": Marco Rubio Eyes Trump's India Visit "Early Next Year"
content: In an exclusive interview with IANS at the White House, Marco Rubio said he expected to travel to India before the end of the year to prepare for the President's visit.

title: Collapsed Buildings, Debris: Satellite Pics Show Impact Of Venezuela Earthquakes
content: High-resolution satellite imagery sourced from Vantor confirms the widespread structural damage caused by two deadly earthquakes in Venezuela.

title: Lashkar Proxies Attend Shoaib Akhtar's Brother's Funeral, Shows Video
content: Those who attended Shahid Akhtar's funeral included the leadership of the terror outfit Lashkar-e-Taiba's political wing.

title: "Welcome, But Be Good": Trump Unveils New US Passport With His Photo
content: The image features a glowering Trump leaning on his desk and his signature, with the text of the Declaration of Independence in the background.

title: Kolkata Metro Services Briefly Disrupted After Suicide Attempt At Kalighat
content: The incident occurred around 7:20 am, following which truncated services were operated between Maidan and Dakshineswar, and between Mahanayak Uttam Kumar and Shahid Khudiram stations.

title: 'Can't Afford Rs 8,000 Rent': Yamuna Bazar Demolition Pushes Families To Brink
content: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) carried out a demolition drive in Delhi's Yamuna Bazar area, targeting settlements between Ghat No. 2 and 32 on the Yamuna floodplain.

title: 2 Killed, 8 Injured After Roof Collapse During Muharram Procession In Lucknow
content: Five of the injured were referred to the trauma centre of the King George's Medical University (KGMU) after their conditions were found to be serious.

title: "Lone Voice Of Silence": Sonia Gandhi's Gaza Op-Ed Draws BJP Criticism
content: The BJP accused the Rajya Sabha MP of trying to misinform the people and mixing up foreign policy with vote bank.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: New 4.9 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Venezuela
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Exclusive: A Massacre In Waiting, And A World That Chooses Silence
content: In Sudan, Rapid Support Forces (RSF) militants are closing in on the strategic city of El Obeid. Experts and rights groups have warned of a "genocide-like" event, similar to what unfolded in El Fasher.

title: Tamil Nadu Woman Dies After Fall From Hilltop Temple As Monkeys Surround Her
content: Police said the couple was feeding fruits to monkeys when several of the animals suddenly gathered around them.

title: Terror Plot Averted In Assam, 2 Members Of Banned Outfit ULFA(I) Arrested
content: Security personnel recovered a significant cache of arms, ammunition, and survival equipment from their possession.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: "Violence Will Be Met With Violence," Says JD Vance As US Strikes Iran
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a 14-point deal to end the Middle East war on June 17.

title: Video: US Releases Footage Of Strikes On Iran, Then A Warning By Tehran
content: The US said the strikes on Iran were a "powerful response" to Tehran's attack on a commercial ship in Strait of Hormuz on June 25.

title: Homebuyers can seek relief for delay after possession: Supreme Court
content: the SC set aside an order of national consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) which held that ahomebuyer ceases to be a consumer after taking possession of the flat . the arbitration clause in homebuyer-real estate company agreement would not block the former from approaching the consumer forum to raise grievances .
